author: kevaundray
content:
Was looking at the nethermind code to see what changes are required once the text fixtures have been officially released (next week):

- It seems like you don't use the `keys` field in the executio witness, which is great because the fixtures removes them

- SSZ is required however you have a SSZ library already. From what I can tell, it doesn't work with Lists of Lists, but this can be solved with AI tbh

- In order to ingest the tests, I think you will need to hook up stateless execution to EEST. 

What this would mean is that when you run the EEST tests, it would first validate blocks using a database. Then it would generate the ExecutionWitness for the block, and use that to then validate the block statelessly. The test would then check that the result from stateless validation equals the results from stateful validation

author: kevaundray
content: For the test fixtures, we encoded it using ssz (which is in spirit close to what you've done with the manual encoding) -- I can answer questions on this when the fixtures are released or can point you to the execution specs

author: kevaundray
content: Ah I guess one thing to note, is that none of the above initially requires zkVMs, we would just be testing native execution of the stateless library with EEST, then after we can test riscv emulation of the tests against each zkVM

author: kevaundray
content: Let me know if anything I said above sounds off or is unclear, especially the part about your code, as I could be misunderstanding something!
